seoTitle: Mount Hutton vs Mount Irvine — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Hutton and Mount Irvine. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Hutton vs Mount Irvine: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Hutton and Mount Irvine.
heading: Mount Hutton vs Mount Irvine — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Hutton, New South Wales and Mount Irvine, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Irvine has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Mount Hutton (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Hutton or Mount Irvine safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Hutton has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Mount Irvine scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Hutton scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Irvine scores 20/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Hutton or Mount Irvine?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Irvine has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
